threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their confused look when the 106 bus goes off-route — it’s like a local rite of passage figuring it out.
Caught the N68 last night, two blokes debating if a kebab's worth the midnight trek. One swore it tasted better at 3 AM than any posh restaurant.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One evening, I saw a group of kids playing soccer in the street while their grandmas chatted on the stoops. That mix of generations is classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you watch a DJ spin records at a laundromat while you wait for your clothes. Just another Wednesday night vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're legit when they know not to bother with the 14 at rush hour and have a secret Muni hack for the hidden routes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a kid on the 24th St. bus doing a magic trick with his phone. He had the whole ride laughing and forgot we were stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how quiet Gràcia's plazas get after 10 PM. It's like a different world, where locals actually unwind away from the bustle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll spot them in Gràcia, clutching a map and looking up at the street signs like they’re in a scavenger hunt. It's cute but a bit sad.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' and have no clue about the bus routes that actually go anywhere.
Caught a guy serenading the night bus crew with a ukulele in Brixton last weekend. Oddly soothing as we rattled by empty streets.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Saw a kid on a bike pulling a mini grill behind him, shouting 'BBQ time!' to his friends. That’s when I knew Neukölln feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you see a random collection of doner shops holding court at a subway station entrance, each claiming to have the best secret sauce.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they sigh when the Muni is late—it's a mix of annoyance and acceptance, like it's a ritual.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy taking a nap on the 47 while nurses in scrubs squeezed in during rush hour—only in SF can the weary and the working coexist so closely.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the park, but miss the real vibe in the small plazas—it's where locals sip their café while kids play, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their unwrinkled metro maps—still pristine, unlike the folded, scribbled ones we all carry.
